message:
feat(runtime): add conformal predictor for frame-time risk bounds

Implement Mondrian (bucketed) conformal prediction for distribution-free
upper bounds on frame time. This provides coverage guarantees without
distributional assumptions, suitable for render budget gating decisions.

Bucket Strategy:
- BucketKey: (ScreenMode, DiffStrategy, cell_count_bucket)
- Cell count buckets: [0-1K), [1K-5K), [5K-20K), [20K-100K), [100K+)
- Separate calibration per bucket for heterogeneous conditions

Conformal Method:
- Nonconformity scores: residuals |actual - predicted|
- Rolling window per bucket (default: 256 samples)
- Quantile-based threshold: Q_{1-α}(residuals)
- Default α = 0.05 for 95% coverage guarantee

Fallback Hierarchy:
1. Use bucket-specific quantile if min_samples met
2. Fall back to mode-aggregated quantile
3. Fall back to global quantile
4. Fall back to q_default (10ms conservative bound)

New Types:
- ConformalConfig: alpha, min_samples, window_size, q_default
- BucketKey: Identifies calibration bucket
- ConformalPredictor: Maintains per-bucket residual history
- ConformalPrediction: Bound with provenance (bucket, quantile, level)
- ConformalUpdate: Encapsulates observation for update

Key Properties:
- Distribution-free: No parametric assumptions required
- Adaptive: Tracks non-stationarity via rolling window
- Explainable: Each prediction includes provenance
- Lightweight: O(window_size) per bucket

Tests:
- Comprehensive unit tests for all bucket combinations
- Coverage validation under simulated drift
- Fallback hierarchy tests

Implements bd-3e1t.3.2 (conformal predictor module).
Part of bd-3e1t.3 (Conformal Frame-Time Predictor epic).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
